raw_consumer_statement:
Subject : Formal Complaint Against Citi for Mishandling Credit Card Dispute I am filing this complaint against Citibank regarding their repeated failure to resolve a dispute involving a duplicate and unauthorized charge on my personal credit card from company travel. 

Summary of the Issue : Disputed Amount : {$460.00} Personal Credit Card Ending : XXXX Company Credit Card Ending : XXXX  The charge of {$460.00} was already paid by my employer via my company credit card. I have submitted comprehensive documentation to Citi, including : - My company expense report showing the transaction history - The approved and reimbursed expense report - Proof of payment which includes the disputed charge - Proof of the duplicated charge on my personal credit card statement - A written letter explaining the circumstances Despite this, Citi has rejected my dispute XXXX times, continuing to claim that I owe the amount on my personal card. This is a duplicate charge and was not authorized for personal billing. 

Summary of Actions Taken : Submitted dispute documentation to Citi on XXXX separate occasions, beginning on XX/XX/year>, through XX/XX/year>. This included mailing in documentation/ writing a letter attesting to the inaccurate charge, emails with additional documents directly to the " billing disputes department '' and multiple phone calls. With each call, Citi could not understand why my dispute was getting rejected as it was a clear-cut double charge. Each time I resubmitted my dispute, yet again it was rejected. I even had to get a new credit card sent to my home. 

Resolution Requested : Requested that the {$460.00} charge be removed from my personal credit card, as it has already been paid by my company and constitutes a duplicate, unauthorized transaction. 

What Citi has put me through with a simple double charge, makes me never want to use their services again. It is negligent how they are serving their customers, and they have completely mishandled my dispute. Citis repeated denial of this dispute, despite clear documentation, reflects a failure to properly investigate and resolve the issue. I am seeking CFPBs assistance in ensuring this matter is handled appropriately and the charge is reversed. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.
